rationale: Title Y has the cleaner Heart Rhythm style: topic first, comparator clear, study design after the colon, and population placed naturally at the end. Title X is more informative because “Universal Hemolysis” signals a key finding, but that phrase feels slightly overstated and less polished in a journal title.
loser_weakness: Title X is longer and less elegant; “Universal Hemolysis” is precise if fully supported, but it reads somewhat categorical and reduces title polish.
